metadata.source: Per-region dossiers (water.md / legal.md); each entry carries its primary water-rights citation where available
metadata.license: Research dossiers internal; underlying primary sources (water-rights statutes, regulator docs) carry their own licenses
metadata.note: Carries red-line underlying data for single-entity water-source control (Overview decision 9). Community-commons regimes (acequia in N New Mexico, comunidade de montes in Galicia, ALR in BC) generally score low risk; prior-appropriation regimes can shut off late-priority holders in drought (Cascadia, Kootenays) which is a different risk profile than single-entity control.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Portuguese public dominion (DPH); ARH regional water authority licences boreholes
properties.control_risk_notes: Water in Alqueva-served areas is contractually allocated; outside Alqueva, boreholes require ARH (regional water authority) authorisation, with state as primary gatekeeper.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: public-utility/state-managed allocation (Alqueva contracts; ARH authorisations)
properties.source: APA Plano de Gestao de Regiao Hidrografica Alentejo (2022-2027); Alentejo legal dossier
properties.gaps: Dossier does not name a single private/corporate operator controlling supply; precise allocation rules of Alqueva concession not detailed.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Spanish public dominion; Plan Hidrologico Galicia-Costa; community traidas vecinales tradition
properties.control_risk_notes: Galicia's community-water tradition (traidas vecinales) is a culturally established and legally recognised model that maps directly onto cohousing and ecovillage water-governance designs.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state hydrological-plan abstraction controls plus communal traida governance
properties.source: Plan Hidrologico Galicia-Costa, third cycle (2023); Community-owned rural water supplies in Galicia (2021)
properties.gaps: Dossier does not quantify share of supply under community vs state regime.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Romanian state water authority (Apele Romane); traditional hand-dug wells and springs at parcel level
properties.control_risk_notes: Traditional rural water access in the region is dominated by hand-dug wells (often 3-8 m deep) and natural springs, both of which remain reliable in most communes.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state-managed via ANM/Apele Romane; localised self-supply via wells and springs
properties.source: ANM Apele Romane water resources reports; ICPDR Romania profile
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail formal water-rights priority or any concession holders.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Irish EPA; private boreholes and springs at parcel level; Uisce Eireann public mains
properties.control_risk_notes: Rainwater harvesting from a 200 m2 roof captures roughly 240,000-480,000 L/year, comfortably above any plausible household demand; spring/well plus rainwater is the canonical self-supply pattern.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: public-utility-managed mains; abundant rainfall removes priority-rationing pressure
properties.source: EPA Ireland Drinking Water Quality Reports; Geological Survey Ireland
properties.gaps: Dossier does not explicitly describe the abstraction-licensing regime.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via NRW abstraction regime; Welsh Water mains; One Planet Development requires on-site supply
properties.control_risk_notes: OPD policy framework actively requires practitioners to engineer rainwater harvesting and on-site wastewater treatment to high standards as part of the planning approval pathway.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: public-utility-managed (Welsh Water) plus mandated on-site self-supply under TAN 6
properties.source: Welsh Government TAN 6 Planning for Sustainable Rural Communities (2010); BGS Hydrogeology of Wales
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail private-ownership concentration in Welsh Water successor structure.

properties.water_rights_regime: French Code de l'environnement public-dominion regime; water rights tied to land (bealieres and spring rights); Parc national constraints
properties.control_risk_notes: Cevennes water-politics are notoriously contested; downstream agricultural and tourist demand drives abstraction conflicts and Parc national des Cevennes biosphere reserve regulations constrain any large-scale abstraction or impoundment.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state prefectoral arretes secheresse plus historic bealiere allocations
properties.source: BRGM Groundwater monitoring; Parc national des Cevennes Charte
properties.gaps: Dossier does not quantify the share of contested abstraction held by single agricultural or tourist operators.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Provincia Autonoma di Bolzano; historic Wasserrechte attached to parcels; Waale gravity-irrigation commons
properties.control_risk_notes: Historic spring or Waale rights are valuable, legally durable, and not transferable without due process; the Waale network is a communally maintained centuries-old irrigation system.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: provincial water-rights administration plus Waale rotational allocations
properties.source: Provincia Autonoma di Bolzano Ufficio Idrografico; EURAC Alpine Environment Institute
properties.gaps: Dossier does not enumerate concentration risk in provincial hydropower concessions.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Confederacion Hidrografica del Cantabrico (Spanish state public dominion)
properties.control_risk_notes: Spain's Third Cycle Hydrological Plans for the Cantabrian basin (2022-2027) tighten abstraction controls and emphasise ecological-flow protection; abundant precipitation and many small springs reduce concentration risk.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state-managed via Confederacion Hidrografica del Cantabrico ecological-flow priorities
properties.source: Confederacion Hidrografica del Cantabrico; Plan Hidrologico Demarcacion Cantabrico Occidental
properties.gaps: Dossier does not discuss municipal water company structures or concessions.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via German Wasserhaushaltsgesetz; Wasserrechte and Brunnenanzeige codified at Land level
properties.control_risk_notes: Groundwater levels are dropping after consecutive dry summers (2018, 2019, 2020, 2022); long-term resilience favours diversified supply (well plus roof catchment plus dedicated greywater recycling), and rural Saxony-Anhalt regulatory navigation requires fluent local engagement.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state-permit Wasserrechte regime with groundwater-protection priority
properties.source: Umweltbundesamt Water Resources in Germany; DWD
properties.gaps: Dossier does not name large private agricultural irrigators that might concentrate abstraction.

properties.water_rights_regime: EU WFD implementation via Estonian Environment Agency; state-managed public waters under recent forest and water policy
properties.control_risk_notes: Mainland southeast Estonia offers near-textbook freshwater resilience with high precipitation, deep glacial aquifers, abundant springs, and a culturally normal pattern of hand-dug wells and shallow boreholes.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state-administered abstraction permits; Saaremaa limestone karst yields locally variable
properties.source: Estonian Environment Agency Keskkonnaagentuur water resources data
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail formal abstraction-licensing priority or any private concession holders.

properties.water_rights_regime: Oregon prior appropriation doctrine (first in time, first in right)
properties.control_risk_notes: Water rights in Oregon operate under a prior-appropriation doctrine which matters critically for any settlement drawing surface water or large-volume groundwater; historic water rights on farm properties may be appurtenant to the land and are valuable and transferable.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: first-in-time first-in-right; domestic-use exemption up to 15,000 gallons/day under ORS 537.545
properties.source: Oregon Water Resources Department Groundwater Resources (2022); Oregon Revised Statutes 537.545
properties.gaps: Dossier does not name specific senior irrigation district holders in target sub-basins.

properties.water_rights_regime: Riparian-derived common law; Vermont ANR Streamflow Protection Program; lightly regulated spring and well development
properties.control_risk_notes: Spring and well development is lightly regulated in Vermont; rainwater harvesting is legal and common, and water availability is rarely a problem in forests of the humid, temperate northeastern US.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: state in-stream flow protection; abundant precipitation removes priority rationing pressure
properties.source: Vermont Agency of Natural Resources Streamflow Protection Program; USGS Sleepers River FS-166-99 (1999)
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail any large private water-utility concentration.

properties.water_rights_regime: North Carolina riparian rights doctrine; groundwater wells permitted by NC Division of Water Resources with no allocation permit for domestic use
properties.control_risk_notes: NC follows the riparian rights doctrine; landowners with property adjoining a stream have the right to make reasonable use of the water; groundwater allows landowners to drill wells on their own property with no allocation permit required for domestic use.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: riparian reasonable-use rule; no prior appropriation
properties.source: NC Division of Water Resources Water Wells and Groundwater Use; WRI Aqueduct 4.0
properties.gaps: Dossier does not enumerate municipal-utility concession structures around Asheville.

properties.water_rights_regime: Wisconsin riparian rights for surface water; groundwater well permits via WI DNR; karst aquifer at parcel scale
properties.control_risk_notes: Private wells dominate the rural water-supply picture and use-value assessment plus minimal allocation barriers leave control highly distributed; primary risk is karst-conveyed nitrate from neighbouring CAFOs rather than corporate water capture.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: riparian reasonable-use rule plus state well-permitting; spring-fed cold-water streams maintain year-round flow
properties.source: Wisconsin Groundwater Coordinating Council 2024 Report; USGS streamflow trends
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail formal abstraction-licensing priority for large irrigators.

properties.water_rights_regime: Arkansas riparian doctrine for surface water; groundwater regulated by Arkansas Natural Resources Commission with minimal permitting for domestic use
properties.control_risk_notes: Karst aquifers respond to land use changes kilometres away, and conventional farming or development upstream can reach a spring head within years; CAFO contamination (nitrates, E. coli) is documented in northwest Arkansas karst systems near Benton/Washington counties.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: riparian reasonable-use rule; rainwater harvesting unlimited; no statewide volume cap
properties.source: USGS Professional Paper 1854 (2024); USGS Springs of the Ozark Physiographic Province
properties.gaps: Dossier does not name specific corporate CAFO operators or quantify their share of abstraction.

properties.water_rights_regime: Prior appropriation doctrine; acequia community commons under NM Stat. 73-2-28; NM Office of the State Engineer adjudication
properties.control_risk_notes: Acequia associations have statutory authority to veto water right transfers into or out of their ditch systems; acequia culture is the deepest living expression of commons-based water management in North America, governed by elected mayordomo and commissioners.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: first-in-time first-in-right plus rotational acequia limpia; Upper Rio Grande fully appropriated with junior rights curtailed first
properties.source: New Mexico Statutes 73-2-28 (2025); NM Office of the State Engineer Water Rights Division; New Mexico Water Advocates (2025)
properties.gaps: Dossier does not assess specific risk of senior-rights consolidation by external buyers within named acequias.

properties.water_rights_regime: Nova Scotia Water Resources Protection Act (2019, consolidated); provincial licence required above 23,000 L/day; private wells dominant
properties.control_risk_notes: Surface water rights are administered under the Water Resources Protection Act (2019) and taking water from surface sources requires a provincial licence for volumes exceeding 23,000 L/day; 42% of Nova Scotians rely on private wells, leaving control highly distributed.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: provincial licence threshold plus emerging mandatory water conservation orders (2024 drought)
properties.source: Nova Scotia Government Surface Water and Groundwater Availability resource pages
properties.gaps: Dossier does not name any large industrial or agricultural licensees that might concentrate abstraction.

properties.water_rights_regime: BC Water Sustainability Act (2016); prior-appropriation licences administered provincially
properties.control_risk_notes: Water licences are the second trap: Kootenay rivers and streams have existing licence holders (irrigation districts, municipalities, industry), and late-priority licences can be shut off in drought years.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: first-in-time first-in-right under Water Sustainability Act; provincial drought-level curtailment of junior licences
properties.source: BC Water Sustainability Act 2016; BC Drought Information Portal; Kootenays legal dossier
properties.gaps: Dossier does not enumerate specific senior licensees by sub-basin.

properties.water_rights_regime: Quebec public-dominion water under MELCCFP; Quebec Water Strategy 2018-2030; municipal supply from Lake Memphremagog
properties.control_risk_notes: Lake Memphremagog is the primary drinking-water source for Sherbrooke (population ~172,000); municipal water security is classified as high by the Quebec Ministry of Environment; rural settlements typically use private fractured-rock wells with high success rates.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: public-utility-managed reservoirs (18 managed reservoirs in Saint-Francois watershed); abundant precipitation minimises priority rationing
properties.source: MELCCFP Quebec's Water resource page; Quebec Water Strategy 2018-2030
properties.gaps: Dossier does not detail private bottled-water or agricultural-irrigation concession holders.

properties.water_rights_regime: Communal water under ejido and usos y costumbres; Conagua federal licensing in private-title contexts; no formal water rights in most comunal territories
properties.control_risk_notes: Water use is governed internally by ejido/comunal assembly rules, not by Conagua licences, which can be an advantage or a vulnerability depending on community relations; cloud-forest catchments at elevation yield year-round spring flow.
properties.drought_priority_mechanism: comunal assembly allocation in usos-y-costumbres territories; Conagua licences elsewhere; gravity-fed spring systems with 90-day reservoir capacity as baseline
properties.source: Conagua Statistics on Water in Mexico (2017 edition); SIPAZ Facts about Oaxaca
properties.gaps: Dossier does not document specific cases of corporate water capture in highland comunas; Atoyac contamination affects valley supply but not directly described as single-entity control.
